channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇱🇦🇪 Israel Deploys Iron Dome Systems and Personnel to UAE
item.description: Israel sent Iron Dome air-defense batteries and operators to the UAE during the Iran war, marking the first publicly acknowledged Israeli military deployment to the Emirates.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇮🇱 EU Sanctions Hamas Leaders and Israeli Settler Organizations
item.description: European Union foreign ministers unanimously agreed to sanction Hamas leaders and Israeli settler groups including Amana, Nachala, and Regavim over West Bank violence and Gaza devastation.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Keir Starmer Faces Deep Political Peril After Surviving Resignation Calls
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer remains under intense pressure despite surviving calls for his resignation, as internal Labour divisions and policy controversies continue to mount.

item.title: 🌍 Global Conflict Displacements Hit Record High in 2025
item.description: Internal displacements caused by violence and conflict reached record levels in 2025, driven by wars in Ukraine, Sudan, Gaza, and other conflicts worldwide.

item.title: 🇺🇸💰 US Inflation Jumps to 3.8% as Iran War Drives Energy Costs Higher
item.description: US consumer prices rose 3.8% annually as the Iran conflict disrupted oil supplies and pushed energy costs higher, complicating Federal Reserve policy decisions.

item.title: 🇷🇸 Serbia Hosts First Joint Military Exercise With NATO
item.description: Serbia conducted its first joint military drills with NATO, less than 30 years after the alliance bombed Belgrade during the Kosovo war, marking a significant shift in Belgrade's foreign policy alignment.

item.title: 🏦 Kevin Warsh Confirmed to Federal Reserve Board in Close Senate Vote
item.description: The US Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh to the Federal Reserve Board in a narrow vote, with Democratic Senator John Fetterman joining Republicans to advance the nomination.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇦🇪 Israel Sent Iron Dome Batteries and Personnel to UAE, US Envoy Says
item.description: US Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee confirmed Israel deployed Iron Dome air defense systems to the UAE amid ongoing regional tensions with Iran.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US Developing Contingency Plans for Iran War, Hegseth Tells Congress
item.description: Pentagon Chief Pete Hegseth told Congress the US has plans for both escalation and withdrawal from the Iran conflict as the war's price tag nears $29 billion.

item.title: 🇸🇦🇮🇷 Saudi Arabia Covertly Struck Iran During War, Sources Say
item.description: Saudi Arabia secretly conducted military strikes against Iran during the conflict, marking a significant escalation in regional involvement against Tehran.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇵🇰 China Presses Pakistan to Deepen Iran Mediation Ahead of Trump-Xi Talks
item.description: Beijing is urging Pakistan to take a larger role in mediating with Iran as President Trump prepares to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Defense Industry Frustrated by War Demands and Coordination Gaps
item.description: European defense companies struggle to meet surging production demands amid bureaucratic hurdles and fragmented procurement across member states.

item.title: 🇺🇸 All Allies Surpassed US Military Budget Purchasing Power Last Year
item.description: A new report shows US allies now collectively outspend America in military purchasing power, reflecting shifting global defense dynamics.

item.title: 🇺🇦 US Ukraine Weapons Policy Uncertain Under Trump, Report Says
item.description: Washington Post reports on uncertainty in US weapons policy toward Ukraine as the Trump administration reviews its approach to the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Why Iran Is Increasingly Targeting the UAE in Its War Messaging
item.description: Iran has intensified propaganda targeting the UAE, highlighting regional tensions and Tehran's strategic calculations in the Gulf.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇦 Russia-Ukraine War Latest Developments
item.description: Ongoing coverage of the Russia-Ukraine conflict with the latest military and diplomatic developments.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Starmer Resignation Calls Grow Amid Political Crisis
item.description: BBC reports on growing calls for UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer to resign amid mounting political challenges.

item.title: 🇻🇪🇬🇾 Venezuela-Guyana Dispute Over Essequibo Reaches International Court
item.description: Venezuela's territorial claim over Guyana's Essequibo region continues at the International Court of Justice amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin Hails Russia's Test of New Nuclear-Capable ICBM
item._description: Russia tested what Putin called the world's most powerful nuclear-capable intercontinental ballistic missile, raising military tensions. Military Tue, 13 May 2026 01:03:00 +0300 South China Morning Post

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Finalizes $355 Billion in Refunds for Illegal Trump Tariffs
item.description: The US government finalized plans to refund $355 billion collected from tariffs deemed illegal during the Trump administration.

item.title: 🇰🇼 Kuwait Says It Foiled Iranian IRGC Infiltration Operation
item.description: Kuwait announced it disrupted an Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps infiltration attempt on Bubiyan Island amid heightened regional tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Iran War Price Tag Reaches $29 Billion, Pentagon Says
item.description: The Pentagon updated the cost of the Iran conflict to $29 billion, up from $25 billion just two weeks ago.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Prime Minister Resignation Crisis Deepens
item.description: BBC reports on the deepening political crisis surrounding UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Lebanon Paramedics Among 13 Killed in Israeli Strikes
item.description: Lebanon reported that two paramedics were among 13 people killed in Israeli strikes, escalating regional tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Corruption Probe Escalates as Zelensky's Ex-Chief of Staff Faces Court
item.description: Ukraine's anti-corruption investigation reached former chief of staff to President Zelensky as the probe intensifies.
